title: Diplazium neglectum
taxonomy: Diplazium neglectum is classified within the family Athyriaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as lady ferns or lady-fern-like ferns. This classification places Diplazium neglectum in the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. intro: Diplazium neglectum, also known as the neglected shield fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Diplazium neglectum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
